title: Banana cream pie
subtitle: Vanilla pastry cream with sliced bananas in a shortcrust case, topped with whipped cream
excerpt: A blind-baked shortcrust pastry case filled with layers of sliced fresh banana and thick vanilla pastry cream, topped with lightly whipped double cream. A classic American diner pie served cold.
sourceNotes: Banana cream pie is a classic of American diner and home baking, widely documented from the early twentieth century. The combination of a baked pastry case, vanilla pastry cream, and fresh banana slices is standard across American cookbooks and bakery traditions. The recipe here follows the classic approach: blind-baked shortcrust, a cooked vanilla pastry cream allowed to cool and set, fresh bananas, and whipped cream on top.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry case and pastry cream can both be made the day before. Assemble the pie no more than 3 hours before serving; the bananas discolour if left longer.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for blind baking. Fan at 170°C.

glossaryTerms.definition: Baking a pastry case without a filling, using a layer of baking paper and baking beans (or dried pulses) as a weight to keep the pastry flat during the initial bake. Once the sides are set, the beans and paper are removed and the base is returned to the oven to dry out.

A thick, cooked egg-and-milk custard set with cornflour. The cornflour prevents curdling and produces a custard thick enough to slice. It must be cooked until large, slow bubbles appear — undercooking leaves a raw-starch flavour.

content.text: Banana cream pie depends on two things being right: a properly
case (crisp, not soggy beneath the cream) and a fully cooked
that is thick enough to hold its shape when sliced. Assemble no more than 3 hours before serving; the bananas discolour quickly in contact with the cream.
Make and blind bake the pastry case

Rub the butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk and water and bring the dough together. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 190°C conventional. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Press into the edges, letting any excess overhang. Prick the base. Line with baking paper and fill with baking beans. Bake for 20 minutes, remove the beans and paper, then bake for a further 8 to 10 minutes until the base is dry and pale gold. Trim the edges. Cool completely.

Whisk the egg yolks, sugar, and cornflour until smooth. Bring the milk to a simmer, pour a third over the egg mixture while whisking, then return to the pan and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thick and large bubbles appear. Remove from heat, stir in the butter and vanilla. Press cling film directly onto the surface and refrigerate until cold and set.

Beat the cold pastry cream briefly to loosen it. Spread a third of the pastry cream over the base of the cooled pastry case. Arrange a layer of sliced bananas over the cream. Add the remaining pastry cream and smooth the top. Arrange another layer of banana slices over the top. Spoon or pipe the lightly whipped cream over the bananas. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. Cut with a sharp knife dipped in hot water between slices.
